summary.en: The urtype multi-sided platform: a payment network that brings together cardholders and merchants, plus the issuing and acquiring banks that serve them. Founded as BankAmericard 1958 → Visa 1976. Operates on the classic asymmetric MSP pricing — cardholders pay ~$0 per transaction (often rewarded), merchants pay ~1.5-3% interchange. As of 2024 Visa processes ~$15 trillion in payments volume across ~4.5 billion cards and ~150 million merchant locations. The platform predates the language used to describe it and remains the textbook non-tech MSP — useful as a cross-check when teaching the pattern, since the digital examples (Google, Apple) can make MSPs feel like a 21st-century thing.
summary.zh: 教科书式的多边平台原型:一个支付网络把持卡人、商户、以及服务它们的发卡行和收单行连在一起。1958 年作为美国银行卡(BankAmericard)起步,1976 年改名 Visa。运行在经典的不对称 MSP 定价机制上 —— 持卡人每笔支付几乎不付费(经常还能拿返现),商户付约 1.5-3% 的回扣。截至 2024 年,Visa 处理约 15 万亿美元支付额、覆盖约 45 亿张卡、约 1.5 亿商户网点。这个平台比给它命名的术语还早出现,至今仍是非科技领域的教科书 MSP —— 当教这个模式时,Visa 是必要的对照,因为数字时代的例子(Google、Apple)容易让人误以为「多边平台」是 21 世纪的概念。

sources.label: Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Wiley · 2010 (pp. 76-81)
Eisenmann, Parker & Van Alstyne · 'Strategies for Two-Sided Markets' · Harvard Business Review · October 2006
David S. Evans & Richard Schmalensee · Paying with Plastic · MIT Press · 2005

headline.en: The non-tech platform that proves multi-sided logic is older than apps
headline.zh: 证明多边平台并不始于 App 的非科技原型

whyOpen.en: Visa is the cleanest way to learn asymmetric platform pricing: cardholders, merchants, issuing banks, and acquiring banks all need different incentives.
whyOpen.zh: Visa 是学习不对称平台定价最清晰的案例：持卡人、商户、发卡行和收单行需要不同激励。

audience.en: Readers who need a platform example beyond marketplaces and social apps.
audience.zh: 适合需要跳出市场平台和社交 App、理解平台本质的读者。

commonMisreads.en: Visa is not just a card brand; the strategic asset is a governed acceptance network with asymmetric incentives.
commonMisreads.zh: Visa 不只是银行卡品牌；战略资产是一个有治理、有不对称激励的受理网络。

practicePrompts.en: Which side of your network should pay less because its presence creates value for every other side?
practicePrompts.zh: 你的网络中哪一边应该少付费，因为它的存在会为其他边创造价值？
